finds_compelling: eyewitness testimony ('I saw with my own eyes')
parallel-structure verdicts that grant virtue before naming vice
the karamat of a living Sufi saint as admissible evidence
citations to a named shaykh, qadi, or fatwa
concrete ethnographic particulars — coinage, dress, diet, funerary rites
comparison of one Muslim jurisdiction against another
hospitality extended by the 'ulama as confirmation of legitimacy
prophecies and dreams that retrospectively organize a life
resists: relativism that treats customs as equally valid regardless of shari'a
abstract theorizing unanchored in witnessed practice
bid'a — religious innovation without prophetic precedent
women's public visibility and independence contra male guardianship
ruler conduct styled 'cruelty' when Islamic law permits the authority
purely commercial motivation presented as sufficient explanation
dismissal of the marvelous as mere fable
criticism of slavery or concubinage that exceeds what fiqh prescribes

citations.author: Ibn Battuta (trans. H.A.R. Gibb)
citations.work: The Travels of Ibn Battuta, A.D. 1325-1354

citations.author: Tim Mackintosh-Smith
citations.work: Travels with a Tangerine: A Journey in the Footnotes of Ibn Battuta
